zh.lifeSummary: 當John Saint John於1225年出生在英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛時，他的父親Robert Saint John26歲，母親Agnes de Cantilupe26歲。他於1256年6月29日與Alice FitzPiers在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1302年9月29日在英國英格蘭哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛享年77歲，埋葬在英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Ancar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Alphonse, Etienne, Fabien, Mireille.

Probably an altered form of French (Pas-de-Calais, Nord) Ancart, itself a variant of Hancart and Hanecart: from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Han, a short form of French Jehan and Flemish Johan(nes) (see John ).

variants.name: Bongiovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Antonio, Dante, Salvatore, Santo, Antonino, Domenic, Gaetano, Geno, Giacomo, Gilda, Luciano, Nino.
Italian: from a personal name composed of the elements bon ‘good’ + Giovanni, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Di Giovanna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Alfonso, Carlo, Domenic, Gaetano, Liborio, Nunzio, Pietro, Rosario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name Giovanna, an equivalent of Joan (see John ).

variants.name: Anes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Burhans
meanings: German: from Middle Low German būr ‘farmer, peasant’ + the personal name Hans, a short form of Johannes (see John ), hence a distinguishing occupational name ‘farmer John’ with Hans being a very frequent name. See also Burian .

variants.name: Breihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: distinguishing nickname for a (beer) brewer, from Middle High German briuw(er) ‘brew(er)’ + a short form of the personal name Johann(es) (see John ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

variants.name: Di Giovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Gino, Giuseppe, Guido, Agostino, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Giovanni, an equivalent of John . Compare De Giovanni , Dejohn , and Giovanni .

variants.name: Eanes
meanings: English (Hampshire): probably a variant of Heanes with loss of initial H-, itself a variant of Heane, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a nickname from Middle English hen(e), heine ‘poor, needy, wretched’ (Old English hēan). Eanes is now rare in Britain, and in the US is found predominantly in VA.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ene
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Ioan (see John ).
West African (Nigeria): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When John Saint John was born in 1225, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England, his father, Robert Saint John, was 26 and his mother, Agnes de Cantilupe, was 26. He married Alice FitzPiers on 29 June 1256, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 29 September 1302, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 77, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire, England.

Il a épousé Alice FitzPiers le 29 juin 1256, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1302, à Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Saint John è nato il nel 1225 nel Old Basing, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Saint John, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Cantilupe, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Alice FitzPiers il 29 giugno 1256, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 29 settembre 1302, nel Old Basing, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Saint Johnが1225年にイギリスハンプシュアイアオルド バシングで生まれた時，父Robert Saint Johnは26歳，母Agnes de Cantilupeは26歳であった。1256年6月29日にイギリスイギリスでAlice FitzPiersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1302年9月29日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアオルド バシングで77歳で死去し，イギリスハンプシュアイアオルド バシングスト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire,: 영국 함프실르 드 바징 스트 마이'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: John Saint John(이)가 1225년 영국 함프실르 드 바징에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Saint John(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Cantilupe(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1256년 6월 29일 영국 잉글랜드에서 Alice FitzPiers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1302년 9월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 드 바징에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 함프실르 드 바징 스트 마이'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Saint John nasceu em 1225, em Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Saint John, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Cantilupe, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice FitzPiers em 29 de junho de 1256, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1302, em Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire,: ст мэри'с чурч, олд басинг, хампшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Saint John родился в 1225 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Saint John было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Cantilupe, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice FitzPiers 29 июня 1256 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 29 сентября 1302 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мэри'с чурч, олд басинг, хампшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Saint John nació en 1225, en Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Saint John, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Agnes de Cantilupe, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Alice FitzPiers el 29 de junio de 1256, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1302, en Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en St Mary's Church, Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra.
